purpose: Exact active authority set that can state runtime, package, Provider or backend truth. This set is scanned semantically in full, not limited to documents changed by F151.

documents.disposition: replace active Proxy/kernel/physical worker/package claims; historical statements require explicit retired labeling
section 14.14 closure and historical evidence labeling
single Gateway runtime and direct Provider transport truth
canonical production entry and no Proxy deployment
replace active Gateway-to-Kernel, Kernel-to-Worker and /kernel/* protocol claims with the single Gateway runtime and current Control Plane/API truth; explicit history remains labeled
remove Docker-as-current-last-line-of-defense claims while retaining explicitly historical tradeoff records
remove mandatory/active retired dependencies and backend claims
mark retained history explicitly; current glossary follows direct Provider and single runtime
single Gateway runtime; no physical kernel/worker package; Docker historical projection only
ProviderRouter/direct transports and Provider-to-Gateway zero import
ProviderRoute canonical DTO and direct routing

index_derivation.root_link_extractor: Markdown inline links ending in .md, fragment stripped, resolved relative to root_index, deduplicated in first-seen order

root_candidates.reason: logical Agent/Worker collaboration semantics only; Worker terminology alone is not a physical package, Provider transport, production entry or backend deployment claim
context/harness behavior only; no physical package, Proxy transport or selectable Docker backend declaration
scenario/lane behavior only; current runtime test truth is owned by testing-strategy and implementation index
persisted message/work semantics; logical Worker values are not physical package or backend deployment claims

index_derivation.derivation_gate: re-extract both indexes at verification; any new or reclassified link capable of stating runtime/package/provider/backend truth must be added to documents[] with an exact disposition, otherwise fail

current_truth.provider: ProviderRouter plus typed direct transports; no LiteLLM Proxy production path
current_truth.runtime: one Gateway runtime; Inline/Graph selector semantics do not imply physical kernel/worker packages
current_truth.packages: no apps/kernel, management/kernel, management/worker or workers/* production package
current_truth.backend: new console/event writes are inline; docker is accepted only by historical raw Event decode/projection

semantic_gate.scan_set: all documents[] regardless of whether their bytes changed
semantic_gate.history_allowed: only when the enclosing heading/table row/paragraph explicitly labels the claim historical, retired or no longer active
active_claims_forbidden: LiteLLM Proxy as required/current production component
OctoKernel or Workers as current physical packages
apps/kernel or workers/* as current path
Docker as selectable current execution runtime
semantic_gate.table_rule: a completion/mandatory/current table row with a checkmark or equivalent active status fails even if another paragraph says retired
semantic_gate.diagram_rule: Mermaid/current runtime chain containing a retired component fails unless the entire diagram is explicitly historical
semantic_gate.unlisted_authority_file: fail when the active authority index links a Markdown document capable of stating runtime/package/provider/backend truth but documents[] omits it

negative_fixtures: an explicitly historical retired Proxy paragraph passes
a checked completion table row for an old kernel/worker/Proxy fact fails
a current Mermaid runtime chain through LiteLLM Proxy fails
an authority-index-linked runtime truth Markdown absent from documents[] fails
